stem: Let \( f(x) = \frac{x^{2} - ([[s]])x + ([[p]])}{x - ([[a]])} \). The graph of \( f \) has a removable discontinuity (a hole) at \( x = [[a]] \). Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to [[a]]} f(x) \).
solution: The numerator factors as \( x^{2} - ([[s]])x + ([[p]]) = (x - ([[a]]))(x - ([[b]])) \). Cancelling the common factor \( x - ([[a]]) \) leaves \( x - ([[b]]) \), so \( \lim_{x \to [[a]]} f(x) = [[a]] - ([[b]]) = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is \( a + b \); after cancelling, the surviving factor is \( x - b \), which gives \( a - b \) at \( x = a \).
This is the negative of the correct value (the factors were subtracted in the wrong order).
This doubles the point being approached instead of evaluating the surviving factor.
This doubles the other root instead of computing \( a - b \).
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Let \( f(x) = \dfrac{\sqrt{x + ([[k]])} - [[m]]}{x - ([[c]])} \). This has a removable discontinuity at \( x = [[c]] \). Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to [[c]]} f(x) \).
solution: Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate \( \sqrt{x+[[k]]} + [[m]] \): \( \dfrac{(x+[[k]]) - [[m]]^{2}}{(x-[[c]])(\sqrt{x+[[k]]}+[[m]])} = \dfrac{x - [[c]]}{(x-[[c]])(\sqrt{x+[[k]]}+[[m]])} = \dfrac{1}{\sqrt{x+[[k]]}+[[m]]} \) (using \( [[m]]^{2} = [[k]] + [[c]] \)). As \( x \to [[c]] \) this gives \( \dfrac{1}{2\cdot[[m]]} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This forgets to double \( m \) in the denominator after rationalizing.
This inverts the correct fraction and drops the factor of 2.
This has the wrong sign on the correct value.
This reports \( m \) itself instead of the rationalized limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

stem: Let \( f(x) = \dfrac{x^{2} - ([[s1]])x + ([[p1]])}{x^{2} - ([[s2]])x + ([[p2]])} \). Both the numerator and denominator vanish at \( x = [[a]] \), giving a removable discontinuity there. Evaluate \( \lim_{x \to [[a]]} f(x) \).
solution: Both quadratics share the factor \( x - ([[a]]) \): the numerator is \( (x-([[a]]))(x-([[b1]])) \) and the denominator is \( (x-([[a]]))(x-([[b2]])) \). Cancelling leaves \( \dfrac{x-([[b1]])}{x-([[b2]])} \), so the limit at \( x=[[a]] \) is \( \dfrac{[[a]]-([[b1]])}{[[a]]-([[b2]])} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This inverts the correct ratio.
This subtracts the other roots instead of forming the ratio of surviving factors.
This assumes the surviving factors are always equal.
This assumes the discontinuity leaves a limit of \( 0 \), ignoring the surviving denominator factor.
This has the numerator and denominator of the ratio swapped in sign.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

solution: Clear the complex fraction: \( \dfrac{1}{x} - \dfrac{1}{[[b]]} = \dfrac{[[b]] - x}{[[b]]x} \), so the whole expression is \( \dfrac{[[b]]-x}{[[b]]x(x-[[b]])} = \dfrac{-1}{[[b]]x} \) for \( x \ne [[b]] \). As \( x \to [[b]] \), this gives \( -\dfrac{1}{[[b]]^{2}} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This has the wrong sign after clearing the complex fraction.
This forgets to square \( b \) in the denominator.
This assumes the hole leaves a limit of \( 0 \).
This reports \( -b \) instead of \( -1/b^{2} \).
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.

solution: Using the factoring identity \( x^{n} - a^{n} = (x-a)(x^{n-1} + x^{n-2}a + \cdots + a^{n-1}) \), cancelling \( x - [[a]] \) leaves \( n \) terms that all equal \( [[a]]^{[[n]]-1} \) at \( x = [[a]] \), giving \( [[n]]\cdot[[a]]^{[[n]]-1} = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This multiplies \( n \) by \( a \) instead of by \( a^{n-1} \).
This drops the factor of \( n \) from the count of surviving terms.
This uses \( n-1 \) instead of \( n \) as the multiplying count.
This reports only the exponent, dropping the base entirely.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
This is off by one from the correct limit.
